labels.name: area:data
labels.description: Data model, ingestion, scorebook, or persistence work

milestones.title: M1: Project Setup & Planning
milestones.description: Organize repo, planning docs, GitHub workflow, and architecture decisions.

milestones.title: M2: Backend Platform Foundation
milestones.description: Create backend service skeleton, generic domain model, API boundaries, persistence, and logging.

milestones.title: M3: Ruski Scorebook Ingestion
milestones.description: Build the Ruski game plugin that validates and normalizes the official Excel scorebook.

milestones.title: M4: iOS Tournament Viewer
milestones.description: Build the viewing-first iOS app shell, tournament navigation, and metadata-driven UI.

milestones.title: M5: Match Detail & Live Updates
milestones.description: Render scorecards, box scores, comments, and server-pushed match updates.

milestones.title: M6: v1 Tournament Release
milestones.description: Harden, test, deploy, and prepare the first tournament-viewing release.

issues.title: Finalize GitHub project workflow and issue templates
issues.body:
Set up GitHub issue forms, labels, milestones, and the initial GitHub Issues backlog.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Issue forms exist for feature, task, spike, and bug work.
- Labels and milestones are created in GitHub.
- The docs backlog points to GitHub as the operational source of truth.

issues.title: Replace starter SwiftData sample app structure with Ruski Report app shell
issues.body:
Remove the default Item-based starter UI and establish the app shell for tournament viewing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The app opens to a Ruski Report home screen.
- Starter Item list behavior is removed.
- The project has clear folders for Models, Views, Controllers, Data, and Services.

issues.title: Define generic tournament domain model
issues.body:
Create backend domain types for tournaments, pods, teams, players, matches, brackets, standings, comments, and live update events without hardcoding Ruski-specific stats.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Generic models support tournament viewing and match detail pages.
- Ruski-specific values are represented through game definitions/events.
- Domain model tests cover core relationships.

issues.title: Define game plugin interface
issues.body:
Create the backend interface that game modules use to parse, validate, normalize, and describe game-specific data.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Interface includes gameType, loadDefinition, parseScorebook, validateScorebook, normalizeScorebook, and calculateStats.
- Backend can reject unsupported game types.
- The interface can support Ruski now and Dye later without changing generic tournament APIs.

issues.title: Create Node/TypeScript backend service skeleton
issues.body:
Set up the backend service with routes, controllers, services, repositories, ingestion, games, config, and logging directories.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Backend has a runnable local dev command.
- Health endpoint exists.
- Tests run in CI or locally.
- Public routes are separated from internal service logic.

issues.title: Choose backend web framework and realtime transport
issues.body:
Decide whether the Node/TypeScript backend should use Express, Fastify, NestJS, or another framework, and decide between WebSockets and Server-Sent Events for v1 live updates.

Expected Output:
- Written recommendation in docs/project-management or an ADR.
- Decision includes hosting implications for Raspberry Pi plus tunnel.
- Decision includes iOS client implications.

Timebox: 2 hours

issues.title: Implement structured backend logging and error responses
issues.body:
Add structured logging and standard error response shapes for API and ingestion failures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Logs include upload lifecycle, gameType, validation failures, and realtime publish events.
- API errors return code, message, and details.
- No production code uses ad hoc console logging without the logger abstraction.

issues.title: Design persistence layer for tournament snapshots
issues.body:
Define how normalized tournament snapshots, matches, comments, and upload reports are stored.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Persistence choice is documented as SQLite prototype or Postgres target.
- Repository interfaces hide database implementation details.
- Tournament snapshot save is atomic.

issues.title: Create Ruski game definition config
issues.body:
Create Ruski rules/config metadata for phases, event types, stat labels, scorecard columns, and display rules.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Config defines miss, make, splash-out, guy, tri, di, vom, redemption, and overtime.
- Config documents that di and tri are special misses.
- Scorecard UI labels can be derived from the config.

issues.title: Create Ruski scorebook schema config
issues.body:
Define the expected Excel workbook tabs, scorecard headers, cell regions, and summary tab expectations for the Ruski scorebook.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Required tabs are enumerated.
- Blank Scorecard layout is captured.
- Game sheet validation can detect renamed/missing scorecard headers.

issues.title: Parse official Ruski Excel scorebook
issues.body:
Implement Excel parsing for the official scorebook format.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Parser reads workbook sheets without changing the file.
- Parser identifies summary tabs, Blank Scorecard, scored game tabs, and AllData.
- Parser extracts player names, shot rows, event flags, and box score totals from game sheets.

issues.title: Validate Ruski scorebook before publish
issues.body:
Implement validation that rejects malformed scorebooks before data is published to app users.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Missing required tabs are reported clearly.
- Changed scorecard headers are reported clearly.
- Invalid stat/event rows produce actionable validation errors.
- Validation report is returned to the admin upload flow.

issues.title: Normalize Ruski scorebook into tournament snapshot
issues.body:
Convert validated Ruski workbook data into generic tournament, match, scorecard, box score, standing, and bracket structures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Game tabs become match details.
- Summary tabs become standings/stat views where applicable.
- Di and tri are counted as misses.
- Snapshot output is independent of Excel cell coordinates.

issues.title: Build admin scorebook upload endpoint
issues.body:
Implement POST /admin/tournaments/:year/upload-scorebook?gameType=ruski.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Endpoint accepts workbook upload from an authenticated admin.
- Endpoint routes to RuskiGamePlugin.
- Successful upload stores a tournament snapshot and returns an ingestion report.
- Failed upload does not publish partial data.

issues.title: Implement public tournament API endpoints
issues.body:
Implement read-only endpoints for active tournament, tournament detail, matches, match detail, and supported games.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Endpoints match the design plan.
- List endpoints return lightweight summaries.
- Match detail returns scorecard definition, rows, box score, and phase.
- Responses are documented or covered by tests.

issues.title: Implement realtime tournament update stream
issues.body:
Implement the server-pushed update stream for tournament and match changes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Clients can subscribe to update events.
- Successful scorebook ingestion publishes a tournament update event.
- Disconnects and reconnects are logged.
- Transport choice is documented.

issues.title: Implement iOS API client and repository protocols
issues.body:
Create iOS data-layer protocols and concrete API clients for games, tournaments, matches, comments, auth session, logging, and realtime updates.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Controllers depend on protocols, not concrete clients.
- DTO mapping is outside SwiftUI views.
- Network errors map to app-level errors.

issues.title: Build iOS home screen with official tournament card
issues.body:
Create the viewing-first home screen for Ruski Report.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Home screen loads active tournament summary.
- Tournament card navigates to tournament detail.
- Loading and error states are visible and understandable.
- Guest users can access the screen.

issues.title: Build iOS tournament detail screen
issues.body:
Build the tournament detail experience with tabs or segmented navigation for pods, bracket, matches, and stats.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tournament detail renders data from the API.
- Pods, bracket, matches, and stats are separated into focused views.
- Views do not hardcode Ruski event labels.

issues.title: Build pod standings UI
issues.body:
Render the 8-pod tournament standings in a mobile-readable layout.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each pod displays four teams.
- Seed, record, cup differential, and shooting percentage are shown where available.
- Long team names do not break the layout.

issues.title: Build bracket viewer UI
issues.body:
Render the playoff bracket from generic bracket data.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Bracket rounds and matchups display clearly on iPhone.
- Winners/progression are visible.
- Layout does not depend on Excel cell positions.

issues.title: Build match detail and metadata-driven scorecard UI
issues.body:
Build the match landing page with score header, box score, scorecard, shot/event log, and comments preview.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scorecard columns come from ScorecardDefinition.
- Event labels come from GameEventType.
- Ruski scorecard layout mirrors the traditional spreadsheet structure.
- The screen handles loading and failure states.

issues.title: Connect iOS realtime updates to visible match state
issues.body:
Subscribe to backend realtime events and update currently visible tournament or match data.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Match detail updates after relevant events.
- Tournament lists refresh after tournament-level events.
- Disconnect/reconnect behavior is logged and user-safe.

issues.title: Implement comments read/write flow
issues.body:
Add match-scoped comments without turning the app into a general messaging product.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Users can read match comments.
- Authenticated users can post comments.
- Guest users receive a clear sign-in prompt for posting.
- Comments are scoped to a match.

issues.title: Implement account and guest session model
issues.body:
Define account, guest, and admin session behavior for viewing and comments.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Guest users can view tournament and match data.
- Authenticated users can post comments.
- Admin-only upload endpoints are protected.
- Future GameCenter/TauID integration remains possible.

issues.title: Plan homelab deployment with public tunnel
issues.body:
Document and implement the v1 deployment target for Raspberry Pi plus public tunnel or reverse proxy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Backend can run under Docker or systemd.
- HTTPS public access path is documented.
- Logs and restart behavior are documented.
- Uploaded workbook and database backup approach is documented.

issues.title: Add backend ingestion test coverage using sample scorebook
issues.body:
Use the uploaded 2026 Ruski Stat Sheet as the sample fixture for ingestion tests.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests verify sheet classification.
- Tests verify representative game sheet parsing.
- Tests verify AllData or summary data normalization where used.
- Tests do not mutate the workbook.

issues.title: Add iOS UI tests for tournament viewing flow
issues.body:
Cover the primary viewing path with UI tests.

Acceptance Criteria:
- App opens to home screen.
- User can navigate to tournament detail.
- User can open a match detail screen.
- Error/loading states are testable.

issues.title: Create architecture decision records for backend stack and realtime transport
issues.body:
Write ADRs for Node/TypeScript backend, homelab hosting, and realtime transport choice.

Acceptance Criteria:
- ADRs capture context, decision, alternatives, and consequences.
- Design plan links remain consistent with ADR decisions.

issues.title: Prepare v1 release checklist
issues.body:
Create a release checklist for the first tournament-viewing release.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Checklist covers backend deployment, scorebook upload, app config, smoke tests, logs, backups, and rollback plan.
- Checklist is stored in project-management docs.
